papers.title_en: KARMA: Knowledge graph-based Automated Reasoning Materialization and Alignment
papers.authors: Jinkyeong Choi, Chaebin Jeong, Donghyeon Park
papers.evidence: 生物医学知识图谱结合LLM的对比学习方法，可迁移至冷启动药物相互作用预测
papers.tldr: 模板对比合成存在分辨率不匹配问题，即序列级优化无法聚焦于有判别性的实体槽。KARMA通过枚举知识图谱路径生成槽对齐候选，并采用槽并行对齐（SPA）将偏好监督施加到关键实体槽上。在生物医学、计算机科学和化学基准上，KARMA超越基础LLM和SFT基线，并与序列/令牌级偏好方法相当。该方法有效解决了模板合成中监督分散的问题，提升了对比学习的效率。
papers.abstract_en: Template-based contrastive synthesis is scalable, but its candidates often differ only in a few entity-slots while sequence-level optimization spreads supervision over mostly shared templates. We formalize this as the Resolution Mismatch Problem and propose KARMA, which enumerates schema-constrained paths over domain knowledge graphs and verbalizes them into slot-aligned contrastive candidates. Slot-Parallel Alignment (SPA) then applies a decoupled slot-level objective to route preference supervision to discriminative entity-slots, with slot-aware masked attention serving as an optional packed-evaluation implementation. Across biomedical, computer-science, and chemistry benchmarks, KARMA outperforms base LLM and same-data SFT baselines, and compares favorably with sequence and token-level preference methods.
